cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote a local barbecue restaurant for weekend family dinners using mild-flavor options, clear ordering guidance, and a reservation-first CTA
input.offer_info: signature skewers, non-spicy grilled vegetables and fish, family-friendly table planning, reservation support, no exaggerated ranking claims
input.audience_segment: local families planning a weekend meal with elders, younger adults, and mixed spice tolerance

mustInclude: scene-based opening around taking family members out for barbecue without making the meal feel risky or chaotic
specific menu proof that balances signature grilled items with milder or easier-to-share choices
CTA that asks for dining time, party size, and whether indoor seating or a quieter table is needed

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Drive bookings for after-work friend gatherings and birthday-style barbecue nights with atmosphere proof, ordering structure, and soft reservation messaging
input.brand_tone: lively, credible, and full of everyday hospitality
input.offer_info: signature skewers, beer-friendly sharing dishes, large-table support, consultative reservation flow, no fake scarcity
input.audience_segment: friends, office teams, and casual celebration groups comparing barbecue venues for 4 to 10 people

mustInclude: opening tied to after-work release, group warmth, or avoiding an awkward barbecue gathering
trust details around table fit, serving rhythm, and what should be ordered first for a smoother meal
low-pressure next step asking for date, headcount, and whether the group wants drinks, indoor seating, or celebration support

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write copy for a budget-sensitive barbecue group meal inquiry with unclear headcount, alcohol expectations, and indoor versus outdoor seating preferences
input.offer_info: group meal options under discussion, pricing depends on party size and drinks, several signature items, multiple seating formats
input.audience_segment: office admins and group organizers comparing several barbecue restaurants for a team meal

mustInclude: implicit request for missing headcount, budget, seating, or drinking details
conservative wording around per-person budget range and package scope
scenario-based recommendation instead of a single fixed quote
mustAvoid: claiming every taste preference will be satisfied

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote a neighborhood barbecue restaurant on rainy and cooling evenings when it has strong food proof but limited polished marketing assets
input.offer_info: signature grilled dishes, practical local convenience, ventilation reassurance without absolute claims, soft reservation push, limited photo assets
input.audience_segment: local diners and visiting friends deciding where to eat on short notice after work or during evening weather changes

mustInclude: sensory but grounded language tied to real grilled dishes or evening dining scenes
practical local information such as arrival convenience, seating type, or peak-hour reservation guidance
conservative handling of freshness, smoke, popularity, and weather-trigger language
mustAvoid: absolute claims like every table orders this

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Grow bookings fast for a barbecue restaurant using ranking language, aggressive hype, must-order pressure, and guaranteed queue-free convenience
input.offer_info: high-demand evenings, strong foot traffic, pressure to increase reservations fast, multiple group-dining scenarios
input.audience_segment: cold local traffic comparing multiple barbecue options

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky ranking, queue, and must-order expressions in risk_phrases
reframed urgency into reservation timing or arrival guidance
trust built through menu proof, seating fit, and ordering guidance instead of hype
mustAvoid: number-one or top-ranked claims without evidence

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push immediate conversion for a barbecue restaurant by promising it is not greasy, will not cause internal heat, has the city's lowest price, and always has tables
input.offer_info: pricing-sensitive offer with mixed dining scenarios and regulatory risk around health, price, smoke, and availability claims
input.audience_segment: mixed cold leads asking for quick booking decisions

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of risky health, price, smoke, and availability language
explicit marking of red-flag phrases in risk_phrases
CTA oriented to collecting time, party size, drinking preference, seating needs, and budget before confirming options
mustAvoid: guaranteed table availability or zero-smoke promise
